commit.message:
Fix: Trim Stripe API-nøgle (trailing newline) + fjern test endpoint

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix sniper buying both sides of same game, tighten MLB thresholds

The settlement sniper had no per-game guards — it could buy YES and NO
on the same game every cycle, locking in guaranteed losses (e.g. $79
loss on NYY/PHI today from 110 YES + 120 NO at 163c/pair avg).

Three sniper guards added:
- Never buy both sides of the same game (side lock)
- Cap total snipe contracts at 20 per game
- Respect momentum scanner's sessionBoughtGames dedup

MLB snipe thresholds tightened (baseball comebacks are real):
- OB confirmation: 3+ run lead in 7th → 4+ run lead in 9th
- Score snipe: 5+ in 8th / 4+ in 9th → 6+ in 9th / 5+ in 9th late

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Debug: Test raw fetch vs SDK, check key whitespace

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(peer-review): three bugs found in deep first-principles review

1. state.ts — advanceGate() missing transaction (atomicity bug)

The gate-advance path performed three separate database operations:
  (a) SELECT flywheel_runs to capture previousPhase
  (b) UPDATE flywheel_runs SET phase, gate_passed_at, checkpoint_sha
  (c) INSERT into phase_events (via logEvent)

With no wrapping transaction, a process crash between (b) and (c) would
leave flywheel_runs updated to the new phase but no gate_advanced event
in the audit log. Callers of flywheel replay and the dashboard would see
the phase jump without a matching event, making root-cause debugging
unreliable and the event log an inaccurate source of truth.

Fixed by wrapping all three operations in this.db.transaction()(), which
better-sqlite3 executes atomically under BEGIN IMMEDIATE / COMMIT.

Note: better-sqlite3 is synchronous and single-threaded, so the failure
window is a process crash (SIGKILL, OOM) rather than a concurrent write.
The transaction guarantees both succeed or both roll back.

2. runs.ts — durationStr() returns negative strings on clock skew

new Date(completedAt) - new Date(startedAt) can be negative when:
  - System clock jumps backward between run start and completion
  - Database row is corrupted (e.g. completedAt copied from a different run)
  - Timestamps from two machines with unsynchronized clocks

Without a guard, Math.floor(-45000 / 1000) = -45 and the output is "-45s",
which renders in the `flywheel runs` table as a confusing negative duration.

Fixed with Math.max(0, ...) on the raw millisecond difference. A 0ms
duration ("0s") is a safe sentinel for all out-of-order timestamp cases.

3. state.ts — missing indexes on api_calls and bead_snapshots run_id

The schema had idx_phase_events_run_ts but no indexes on the other two
tables that are queried per-run:

  getTotalCost()       → SELECT SUM(cost_usd) FROM api_calls WHERE run_id = ?
  getApiCalls()        → SELECT * FROM api_calls WHERE run_id = ?
  beadVelocity()       → SELECT * FROM bead_snapshots WHERE run_id = ? LIMIT N
  getBeadSnapshots()   → SELECT * FROM bead_snapshots WHERE run_id = ?

All four perform a full table scan in the absence of an index. For small
datasets (< 10,000 rows) SQLite's page cache makes this imperceptible.
For a long-running autopilot session that logs API calls every few minutes
or a project with hundreds of bead snapshots, getTotalCost() (called on
every status poll) and beadVelocity() (called on every dashboard refresh)
degrade linearly with table size.

Added:
  idx_api_calls_run_id ON api_calls(run_id)
  idx_bead_snapshots_run_id ON bead_snapshots(run_id, captured_at)

The composite (run_id, captured_at) index on bead_snapshots also
accelerates the ORDER BY captured_at DESC LIMIT N in beadVelocity()
without a separate sort pass.

Both use IF NOT EXISTS so they apply to existing databases on next open.

Also: close epic agent-flywheel-console-3qw.6 — all three transport
integration test children (6.1 SSHManager, 6.2 RemoteRunner, 6.3
NtmBridge) completed in this session.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix Grafana alert rules: add reduce step before threshold

Grafana 11 requires a reduce expression (A → B reduce last → C threshold)
between time series queries and threshold conditions. Without it, alerts
fire with "looks like time series data, only reduced data can be alerted on".

Migrate from CRA + Yarn to Vite + npm

- Replace react-scripts with Vite for dev/build/preview
- Replace Yarn with npm (package-lock.json)
- Move index.html to root, load fonts via CSS link (drop webfontloader)
- Env vars: REACT_APP_BUILD_DATE → VITE_BUILD_DATE (import.meta.env)
- tsconfig: target ES2020, moduleResolution bundler, vite/client types
- Delete dead code: ThemeToggle, ThemeContext, themes, reportWebVitals
- Remove unused packages: uuid, react-switch, web-vitals, webfontloader
- Update deps: fontawesome 6.7, react-toastify 11, typescript 5.7
- Configure ESLint independently with @typescript-eslint + react-hooks
- Migrate tests to Vitest with jsdom and globals
- Build time: ~1.5s (was ~30s+ with CRA)

Fix AI masking thread safety, slider buttons, and CSS crash

- Add null checks after ONNX API calls to prevent crashes
- Remove debug PPM/PGM file writes from AI segmentation
- Fix dangling pointer risk: hold cache mutex across parallel mask
  blend loops in improccoordinator and dcrop
- Add Unsafe accessor variants to AIMaskCache for use under held lock
- Hide SpinButton +/- buttons programmatically via gtk_container_forall
  (CSS-only approach insufficient on Windows GTK3)
- Wrap FileCatalog CSS load_from_data in try-catch and remove
  unsupported properties (max-height, -gtk-icon-shadow)

repository.name: Steep

commit.message:
fix(contracts): prevent unnecessary rebuild on every cargo build (#469)

The contracts build script tracked entire directories with
`cargo:rerun-if-changed`, which caused it to re-run on every `cargo
build` because `scarb build` modifies `Scarb.lock` and `target/` within
those directories. This replaces directory-level tracking with recursive
file-level tracking that excludes `Scarb.lock` files and `target/`
directories.

A similar PR ( https://github.com/dojoengine/katana/pull/281 ) was made
to address the same issue. It worked but the fix was done under the
assumption that the `/contracts` directory is a single `Scarb`
workspace. But since #399, that is no longer the case as now it may
include git submodules which are independent `Scarb` workspace by
themselves.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(spinclass): wire hook command to disallow-main-worktree

Replace cmd.go RunE body to use the new sweatfile hierarchy and hook logic:

- Use git.CommonDir() to get main repo root instead of detecting boundary
- Call sweatfile.LoadWorktreeHierarchy() for worktree-aware config cascade
- Pass disallowMainWorktree flag to Run() instead of boundary/allowed/notify
- Remove detectBoundary() function (no longer needed)
- Remove path/filepath import, add git import

The hook command now correctly distinguishes between:
1. Not in a worktree → Run with flag off
2. In a worktree → load hierarchy, check DisallowMainWorktree setting

All tests pass.

Centralize play/pause logic in AudioPlayerContext

Create AudioPlayerContext to own audioRef, isPlaying state, and provide
play/pause/toggle methods with consistent promise error handling. Remove
scattered .play()/.catch() calls across App, Player, LibrarySong. Fix
spacebar scrolling tracklist by preventing default in keydown handler.
Delete unused Theme.ts. Memoize filteredSongs in Library to fix re-renders.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Replace Dependabot with Renovate for dependency management

Renovate provides built-in auto-merge, groups related updates (e.g. all
GitHub Actions in one PR), and better handles major version breaking changes.

Config: auto-merge minor/patch, group CI actions, flag majors for review.
